zone_page_description: You can see here the configuration of the various entries for your domain.
zone_page_description_2: You also have the option to configure these entries to link your domain to your various services (button "Add an entry").
zone_page_view_history: View the history of my DNS zone
zone_page_guide_button_edit_label: How to edit my DNS zone?
zone_page_guide_button_history_label: Manage the history of a DNS zone
zone_page_error_message: An error occurred while retrieving the information for the zone: {{message}}
zone_page_message_no_zone: You do not have a DNS zone attached to this domain name.
zone_page_form_select_title: Select a type of DNS field
zone_page_form_subdomain_helper_cname: To point to the root, use a SVCB record.
zone_page_form_ttl_min_value: The TTL must be at least 60 seconds.
zone_page_form_ttl_max_value: The TTL cannot exceed 86,400 seconds (24 hours).
zone_page_form_hex_valid: Please enter a valid hexadecimal value (characters 0-9, a-f).
zone_page_form_base64_valid: The public key must be base64 encoded (characters A-Z, a-z, 0-9, +, /, =).
zone_page_form_dmarc_rua_valid: The report address must be a mailto URI (e.g. mailto:dmarc@example.com).
zone_page_form_caa_iodef_valid: For the iodef tag, the value must be a URL (mailto: or https://).
zone_page_form_cname_subdomain_no_at: A CNAME record cannot be created at the domain root (@). You can use SVCB pointing.
zone_page_form_dname_subdomain_no_at: A DNAME record cannot be created at the domain root (@). Please enter a valid subdomain.
zone_page_form_dkim_subdomain_invalid: The selector may only contain letters, numbers, and hyphens (e.g. selector1).
zone_page_form_target_host_valid: The target must be a valid hostname.
zone_page_record_explanation_A: The A record allows you to point your domain name to a server by specifying its IPv4 address.
zone_page_record_explanation_AAAA: The AAAA record works like the A record but uses an IPv6 address instead of an IPv4 address.
zone_page_record_explanation_NS: The NS record allows you to delegate the management of a subdomain to another DNS server.
zone_page_record_explanation_CNAME: The CNAME record allows you to create an alias, that is, to point one domain name to another.
zone_page_record_explanation_DNAME: The DNAME record allows you to automatically redirect all subdomains of a zone to another domain.
zone_page_record_short_SVCB: Association to a service (advanced alternative to ALIAS/ANAME)
zone_page_record_short_TLSA: TLS certificate associated with the domain
zone_page_record_description_A: Once the configuration is validated, <bold>{{domain}}</bold> will point to <bold>{{ip}}</bold>.
zone_page_record_description_AAAA: Once the configuration is validated, <bold>{{domain}}</bold> will point to <bold>{{ip}}</bold>.
zone_page_record_description_NS: Once the configuration is validated, <bold>{{domain}}</bold> will be delegated to the DNS server <bold>{{target}}</bold>.
zone_page_record_description_CNAME: Once the configuration is validated, <bold>{{domain}}</bold> will be an alias of <bold>{{target}}</bold>.
zone_page_record_description_DNAME: Once the configuration is validated, all subdomains of <bold>{{domain}}</bold> will be redirected to <bold>{{target}}</bold>.
zone_page_tooltip_subdomain_cname: The name of the alias to create
zone_page_tooltip_subdomain_dname: The zone whose subdomains will be redirected
zone_page_tooltip_ip: The IP address of the destination server
zone_page_tooltip_ns_target: The DNS server responsible for this zone
zone_page_tooltip_cname_target: The domain name to which the alias redirects
zone_page_tooltip_caa_flags: The value is set to 0. It indicates that the certification authority should not issue a certificate if it does not recognise the label
zone_page_tooltip_caa_tag: The type of CAA property (issue, issuewild or iodef)
zone_page_form_label_caa_target: Target (domain name of the certification authority)
zone_page_caa_tag_option_issue_desc: Authorises the certification authority to issue a certificate for this domain name
zone_page_caa_tag_option_issuewild_desc: Authorises the certification authority to issue certificates for all subdomains (wildcard certificate)
zone_page_caa_tag_option_iodef_desc: Address (URL) used by the certification authority to send an error message
zone_page_record_explanation_CAA: The CAA entry allows you to specify which certification authorities are permitted to issue certificates for your domain.
zone_page_record_description_CAA: Once the configuration is validated, only the authority <bold>{{target}}</bold> will be allowed to issue certificates for <bold>{{domain}}</bold>.
zone_page_tooltip_txt_value: The textual content of the record (e.g. domain verification, SPF policy, etc.)
zone_page_record_explanation_TXT: The TXT entry allows you to associate free text with a domain name. It is commonly used for domain verification, email sending policies (SPF, DKIM, DMARC) or other uses.
zone_page_record_description_TXT: Once the configuration is validated, the value <bold>{{target}}</bold> will be associated with <bold>{{domain}}</bold>.
zone_page_record_explanation_NAPTR: The NAPTR (Naming Authority Pointer) entry allows you to define rewriting rules for naming queries. It is used particularly for telephony (ENUM) and service discovery.
zone_page_record_description_NAPTR: Once the configuration is validated, a NAPTR rewriting rule will be created for <bold>{{domain}}</bold>.
zone_page_tooltip_naptr_order: Processing priority of the rule (0-65535). Lower values are processed first.
zone_page_tooltip_naptr_pref: Preference among rules of the same order (0-65535). Lower values are preferred.
zone_page_record_explanation_SRV: The SRV record allows you to specify the location of a service (server and port) for a given protocol. It is used notably for VoIP, instant messaging, or autodiscover.
zone_page_record_description_SRV: Once the configuration is validated, the service will be directed to <bold>{{target}}</bold> on the specified port for <bold>{{domain}}</bold>.
zone_page_tooltip_srv_priority: Server priority (0-65535). Lower values are contacted first.
zone_page_tooltip_srv_weight: Weight for load balancing between servers of the same priority (0-65535).
zone_page_tooltip_srv_port: Port number on which the service is available (0-65535).
zone_page_tooltip_srv_target: The hostname of the server providing the service.
zone_page_record_explanation_SSHFP: The SSHFP record allows you to publish the SSH key fingerprint of a server in the DNS, in order to verify the authenticity of the SSH connection via DNSSEC.
zone_page_record_description_SSHFP: Once the configuration is validated, the SSH fingerprint will be published in the DNS for <bold>{{domain}}</bold>.
zone_page_tooltip_sshfp_algorithm: The SSH key algorithm of the server (RSA, DSA, ECDSA, or ED25519).
zone_page_tooltip_sshfp_fptype: The hash algorithm used to calculate the fingerprint (SHA-1 or SHA-256).
zone_page_tooltip_sshfp_fp: The hexadecimal fingerprint of the server's SSH public key.
zone_page_record_explanation_TLSA: The TLSA record allows you to associate a TLS/SSL certificate with a domain name via DANE, enhancing the security of encrypted connections in addition to certificate authorities.
zone_page_record_description_TLSA: Once the configuration is validated, the TLS certificate information will be published in the DNS for <bold>{{domain}}</bold>.
zone_page_tooltip_tlsa_selector: Part of the certificate to use
zone_page_tooltip_tlsa_cert: The hexadecimal fingerprint or the raw data of the TLS certificate.
zone_page_record_explanation_RP: The RP (Responsible Person) entry allows indicating the person responsible for a domain name by publishing a contact email address and a reference to additional information.
zone_page_record_description_RP: Once the configuration is validated, the contact information will be published in the DNS for <bold>{{domain}}</bold>.
zone_page_tooltip_rp_mbox: The email address of the responsible person in DNS format (the @ is replaced by a dot, e.g., admin.example.com for admin@example.com).
zone_page_tooltip_rp_txt: The hostname of a TXT record containing additional information about the responsible person.
zone_page_record_explanation_SVCB: The SVCB (Service Binding) record associates a domain name with a service endpoint by publishing its connection parameters (ALPN protocol, port, address, …). It allows compatible clients to discover and automatically use the optimal configuration.
zone_page_record_description_SVCB: Once the configuration is validated, the service parameters will be published for <bold>{{domain}}</bold> to <bold>{{target}}</bold>.
zone_page_tooltip_svcb_priority: Service priority. 0 indicates alias mode, any other value activates service mode.
zone_page_tooltip_svcb_target: The hostname of the server providing the service.
zone_page_tooltip_svcb_params: Additional service parameters (e.g., alpn="h2,h3" ipv4hint="127.0.0.1"). Empty if the priority is 0.
zone_page_record_explanation_HTTPS: The HTTPS entry is a specialised type of SVCB, dedicated to HTTPS services. It allows indicating the HTTPS connection parameters (protocols, IP addresses, etc.) directly in the DNS.
zone_page_record_description_HTTPS: Once the configuration is validated, the HTTPS parameters will be published for <bold>{{domain}}</bold> to <bold>{{target}}</bold>.
zone_page_record_explanation_LOC: The LOC entry allows associating geographical coordinates (latitude, longitude, altitude) with a domain name.
zone_page_record_description_LOC: Once the configuration is validated, the geographical coordinates will be associated with <bold>{{domain}}</bold>.
zone_page_tooltip_loc_altitude: Altitude in metres above sea level
zone_page_tooltip_loc_size: Diameter of the sphere encompassing the entity (in metres)
zone_page_form_label_tlsa_usage_0_desc: Validates the certificate via a trusted public certification authority (PKIX)
zone_page_form_label_tlsa_usage_1_desc: Validates the server's certificate via the PKIX chain
zone_page_form_label_tlsa_usage_2_desc: Defines a trust anchor independent of public certification authorities
zone_page_form_label_tlsa_usage_3_desc: Directly associates the domain's certificate without a certification authority
zone_page_warning_MX: Warning: manually editing MX fields may result in loss of emails!
zone_page_record_explanation_MX: The MX (Mail Exchange) record allows you to define the mail servers responsible for receiving emails destined for your domain.
zone_page_record_description_MX: Once the configuration is validated, emails destined for <bold>{{domain}}</bold> will be routed to <bold>{{target}}</bold>.
zone_page_tooltip_mx_priority: Priority of the mail server (0-65535). Lower values are contacted first.
zone_page_tooltip_mx_target: The hostname of the mail server that will receive the emails.
zone_page_record_explanation_DKIM: The DKIM (DomainKeys Identified Mail) record allows you to authenticate emails sent from your domain. Enter the selector provided by your email provider along with the associated public key.
zone_page_record_description_DKIM: The record will be published under <bold>{{selector}}._domainkey.{{domain}}</bold>.
zone_page_info_DKIM: If you wish to use advanced settings, you can use the advanced editing mode.
zone_page_tooltip_dkim_value: The full value of the DKIM record, including the public key (e.g. v=DKIM1; k=rsa; p=MIGf...).
zone_page_tooltip_subdomain_dkim: DKIM selector. The DNS name will be in the form selector._domainkey.example.com.
zone_page_tooltip_dkim_k: Type of cryptographic key used for the DKIM signature.
zone_page_tooltip_dkim_p: The public key encoded in base64 used to verify DKIM signatures.
zone_page_tooltip_dkim_status: Indicates whether the DKIM key is active or revoked. A revoked key publishes an empty p= field.
zone_page_tooltip_dkim_test_mode: When enabled (t=y), it indicates to receiving servers that the domain is testing DKIM and that messages should not be rejected.
zone_page_tooltip_dkim_subdomain_restric: When enabled (t=s), the key can only be used for the exact domain, not for its subdomains.
zone_page_tooltip_dkim_h: Acceptable hash algorithm(s) for signature verification.
zone_page_tooltip_dkim_s: Service types to which this DKIM key applies.
zone_page_tooltip_dkim_g: Local part of the email address allowed to use this key (e.g. * for all).
zone_page_tooltip_dkim_n: Human-readable notes, not interpreted by systems.
zone_page_record_explanation_DMARC: The DMARC entry allows you to define the email authentication policy for your domain by specifying how to handle messages failing SPF and DKIM checks.
zone_page_record_description_DMARC: Once the configuration is validated, the DMARC policy will be applied to emails sent from <bold>{{domain}}</bold>.
zone_page_record_explanation_SPF: The SPF (Sender Policy Framework) entry allows you to specify which servers are authorised to send emails on behalf of your domain, in order to combat spoofing.
zone_page_record_description_SPF: Once the configuration is validated, the SPF policy will be applied to emails sent from <bold>{{domain}}</bold>.
zone_page_info_SPF: If you wish to use advanced settings, you can use the advanced editing mode.
zone_page_tooltip_dmarc_p: Policy to apply to non-compliant emails
zone_page_tooltip_dmarc_pct: Percentage of messages subject to the DMARC policy (0-100). By default: 100.
zone_page_tooltip_dmarc_rua: URI address for receiving DMARC aggregate reports (e.g., mailto:dmarc@example.com).
zone_page_tooltip_dmarc_sp: Policy to apply specifically to subdomains. If not specified, the policy of the main domain applies.
zone_page_tooltip_dmarc_aspf: SPF alignment mode: R (Relaxed, by default) or S (Strict).
zone_page_tooltip_dmarc_v: Version of the DMARC protocol. Currently, the only valid value is DMARC1.
zone_page_form_spf_subdomain_tooltip: The SPF field is generally configured at the root of the domain (@). Enter @ for the root or a specific subdomain.
zone_page_form_spf_includes_tooltip: Domains whose mail servers are authorised to send emails on behalf of your domain.
zone_page_form_spf_ip4_tooltip: IPv4 addresses or CIDR subnets authorised to send emails (e.g: 203.0.113.0/24).
zone_page_form_spf_ip6_tooltip: IPv6 addresses or CIDR subnets allowed to send emails (e.g.: 2001:db8::/32).
zone_page_form_spf_policy_tooltip: Defines the action to be taken when an unauthorised server attempts to send an email on behalf of your domain.
zone_page_form_spf_include_invalid: Please enter a valid domain name.
zone_page_form_spf_ip4_invalid: Please enter a valid IPv4 address (e.g.: 203.0.113.0/24).
zone_page_form_spf_ip6_invalid: Please enter a valid IPv6 address (e.g.: 2001:db8::/32).
zone_page_form_svcb_params_priority_zero: If the priority is 0, the parameters field must remain empty.
zone_page_resume_info: You are about to add the following entry to your DNS zone:
zone_page_resume_alert: The addition will be immediate in the DNS zone, but please take into account the propagation time (maximum 24h).
zone_page_resume_check_warning_info: Error: Unable to save the DNS configuration. Please check that:
zone_page_resume_check_warning2: The subdomain is not already used by a CNAME record. A CNAME record is not allowed to coexist with other fields on the same subdomain.
zone_page_resume_check_warning3: The CNAME record is not added on the root domain. We remind you that the root domain is the base domain you have registered.
zone_page_form_naptr_flag_help: Indicates the type of processing to be performed (leave empty if no terminal processing is required)
zone_page_form_naptr_regex_help: Rewrite expression applied to the input URI (format: !pattern!replacement!)
zone_page_form_naptr_replace_help: Domain name to delegate the following resolution to (e.g. _sip._udp.example.com.)
zone_page_form_naptr_flag_valid: The flag must be a unique alphanumeric character.
zone_page_form_naptr_service_valid: The service must adhere to the protocol+rs format (e.g. SIP+D2U).
zone_page_form_naptr_regex_valid: The regular expression must not contain quotes or spaces.
zone_page_form_naptr_replace_valid: The replacement must be a valid point or domain name.
zone_page_form_bind_paste_description: Enter a record in DNS format (e.g. www 3600 IN A 192.168.1.1) to add it directly.
zone_page_form_bind_error_invalid_format: The record format is invalid. Check the syntax.
zone_page_form_bind_error_unsupported_ty: This type of record is not supported.
zone_page_form_bind_error_invalid_rdata: The record data (RDATA) is incomplete or invalid.
zone_page_form_bind_error_multiline: Please enter only one record. To add multiple records simultaneously, use the text editing mode.
zone_page_form_add_record_success: Your record has been successfully added.
zone_page_form_add_record_error: An error occurred while adding the record: {{error}}
zone_page_form_cname_already_exists: A CNAME record already exists for this subdomain. You cannot add another record to this subdomain.
zone_page_form_modify_record_success: Your record has been successfully modified.
zone_page_form_modify_record_error: An error occurred while modifying the record: {{error}}
zone_history_description: View the history of changes to your DNS zone and restore a previous version if necessary.
zone_history_error: An error occurred while retrieving the history: {{message}}
zone_history_restore_success: The restoration of your DNS zone is in progress. Propagation will be effective according to the duration of the TTL (maximum 24h). You can track its progress from the ongoing operations page. <Link />
zone_history_restore_error: An error occurred during the restoration: {{message}}
zone_history_restore_modal_description: Are you sure you want to restore this version of the DNS zone from {{date}}? This action will replace the current configuration.
zone_history_restore_modal_propagation_i: The reset in the DNS zone will take a few moments, but please consider the propagation time (maximum 24h).
zone_history_restore_no_permission: You do not have permission to restore the zone, please contact your administrator
zone_page_modify_ttl_info: The TTL corresponds to the maximum time (in seconds) for the propagation of the DNS zone update.
zone_page_modify_ttl_heading: You are about to modify the default TTL value of the zone <bold>{{zone}}</bold>
zone_page_modify_ttl_help_1: Recommended value in case of frequent changes: 3,600 seconds.
zone_page_modify_ttl_value_conditions: The value must be an integer between {{min}} and {{max}}.
zone_page_modify_ttl_propagation_info: The TTL change will take effect after a maximum propagation time of {{seconds}} seconds.
zone_page_modify_ttl_success: The zone's TTL has been successfully updated. The change will take effect after a maximum propagation time of 24 hours.
zone_page_modify_ttl_error: An error occurred while changing the TTL: {{message}}
zone_page_reset_warning: Important! This will delete all existing DNS records and reset the DNS zone to its initial state.
zone_page_reset_propagation_info: The reset will be immediate in the DNS zone, but please take into account the propagation time (maximum 24 hours).
zone_page_reset_minimized_question: Do you want to enable minimal entries when resetting your DNS zone?
zone_page_reset_minimized_no: No, but I want to reset my DNS zone.
zone_page_reset_minimized_yes: Yes, I want to reset my DNS zone with minimal entries.
zone_page_reset_type_a_label: Please choose where your domain should point:
zone_page_reset_type_mx_label: Please choose which server your emails should be routed to:
zone_page_reset_type_custom_mx_label: Specify a mail server and its priority:
zone_page_reset_success: Your DNS zone reset is in progress. Please take into account the propagation time (maximum 24h).
zone_page_reset_error: An error occurred during the reset: {{message}}
zone_page_modify_textual_description: Enter below the text corresponding to the DNS zones you wish to import:
zone_page_modify_textual_propagation_inf: The configuration will be imported in a moment, but please take into account the propagation time (maximum 24h).
zone_page_modify_textual_success: The configuration will be imported in a moment (maximum propagation time of 24h). You can track the progress of the DNS zone import from the ongoing operations page. <Link />
zone_page_modify_textual_error: An error occurred during the import of the configuration: {{message}}
zone_page_modify_textual_error_regex: Warning, the TTL specification is incorrect. The content must start with $TTL followed by a numeric value.
zone_page_modify_textual_dropzone_label: Drop a zone file here or
zone_page_add_entry_modal_heading: Add an entry to the DNS zone
zone_page_add_entry_modal_step_1_select_: Select a type of DNS field
zone_page_add_entry_modal_step_1_expert: Please specify the value for this field:
zone_page_add_entry_modal_step_2_ttl_min: The TTL must be at least 60 seconds.
zone_page_add_entry_modal_step_2_record_: The field {{recordType}} currently generated is as follows:
zone_page_add_entry_modal_step_2_target_: The target must be a valid IPv4.
The target must be a valid IPv6.
The target must be a valid hostname.
zone_page_add_entry_modal_step_2_dkim_t_: The public key is not valid for subdomains of this domain
The public key is valid for subdomains of this domain
zone_page_add_entry_modal_step_2_dkim_pu: The public key must be a valid base64 string (min. 32 characters) or revoke the key.
zone_page_add_entry_modal_step_2_dkim_gr: The granularity must contain only alphanumeric characters and allowed symbols.
zone_page_add_entry_modal_step_2_dkim_no: Notes must not contain the characters = ; "
zone_page_add_entry_modal_step_2_subdoma: Warning: a target is already configured for this domain.
zone_page_add_entry_modal_step_2_mx_help: Warning: manually changing MX fields may result in loss of emails!
zone_page_add_entry_modal_step_2_spf_hel: If your domain {{domain}} is set up on an OVHcloud shared server, the basic SPF field to use is simply:
zone_page_add_entry_modal_step_2_svcb_pr: A priority of 0 indicates alias mode. Any other value corresponds to service mode.
zone_page_add_entry_modal_step_2_svcb_pa: If the priority is equal to 0, this field must remain empty. Otherwise, the format is as follows ipv4hint="127.0.0.1" alpn="h2,h3"
If the priority is 0, the parameters field must remain empty.
zone_page_add_entry_modal_step_3_info: You are going to add the following entry to your DNS zone:
zone_page_add_entry_modal_step_3_alert: The addition will be immediate in the DNS zone, but please take into account the propagation time (maximum 24h).
zone_page_add_entry_modal_step_3_check_w: Error: Unable to save the DNS configuration. Please check that:
The subdomain is not already used by a CNAME record. A CNAME record is not allowed to coexist with other fields on the same subdomain.
The CNAME record is not added on the root domain. We remind you that the root domain is the base domain you have registered.
zone_page_add_entry_modal_spf_senders_ip: Allow the IP of {{serviceName}} to send emails?
zone_page_add_entry_modal_spf_senders_mx: Allow the MX servers of {{serviceName}} to send emails?
zone_page_add_entry_modal_spf_senders_al: Allow all servers whose name ends with {{serviceName}} to send emails? (This option is not recommended)
zone_page_add_entry_modal_spf_other_titl: Do other servers send mail from {{serviceName}}? You can describe them by providing them as arguments to the following fields:
zone_page_add_entry_modal_spf_other_ds_t: Does the mail from {{serviceName}} originally come from other servers belonging to other domains (e.g.: your ISP)?
zone_page_add_entry_modal_spf_all_title: Do the details you have provided describe all hosts that send mail from {{serviceName}}?
zone_page_add_entry_modal_step_2_naptr_r: Leave it blank if the field below is filled in.
Leave it blank if the field above is filled in.
zone_page_add_entry_modal_step_2_naptr_f: The attribute must be a unique alphanumeric character.
zone_page_add_entry_modal_step_2_naptr_s: The service must comply with the protocol+rs format (e.g. SIP+D2U).
zone_page_add_entry_modal_step_2_naptr_r: The regular expression must not contain quotes or spaces.
The replacement must be a valid point or domain name.
zone_page_add_entry_success: The entry has been added to the DNS zone, but please take into account the propagation time (maximum 24h).
zone_page_delete_entry_modal_success: The entry has been removed from the DNS zone, but please take into account the propagation time (maximum 24h).
zone_page_delete_entry_modal_error: An error occurred while deleting the entry {{domain}} from the DNS zone.
zone_page_delete_entry_modal_heading: Remove the entry from the DNS zone
zone_page_delete_entry_modal_warning: The removal will be immediate in the DNS zone, but please take into account the propagation time (maximum 24h).
zone_page_delete_entries_modal_heading: Remove entries from the DNS zone
zone_page_delete_entries_modal_descripti: Do you really want to delete these {{count}} entries from the DNS zone?
zone_page_delete_entries_modal_warning: The removal will be immediate in the DNS zone, but please take into account the propagation time (maximum 24h).
zone_page_delete_entries_modal_success: The {{count}} entries have been removed from the DNS zone, but please take into account the propagation time (maximum 24h).
zone_page_delete_entries_modal_error: An error occurred while deleting the entries from the DNS zone: {{message}}
zone_page_banner_dns_mismatch_title: This DNS zone is not authoritative for your domain name.
zone_page_banner_dns_mismatch_descriptio: This means that your domain name is currently using other DNS servers. Changes made here will therefore not be taken into account.
For these settings to apply, you must configure your domain name to use the OVHcloud DNS servers.
zone_page_banner_zone_status_warning: Your zone has been successfully deployed. However, the following anomalies have been detected:
zone_page_banner_zone_status_error: Our systems have detected errors while verifying your zone. <bold>Your latest changes have not been taken into account.</bold> To remedy this, please correct the following issues:
zone_page_banner_propagation: One or more actions have been recently carried out in the DNS zone. For any addition, modification, deletion, or reset, please note that the change may require a maximum propagation time of 24 hours.
zone_page_banner_empty_zone: There are no records in your DNS zone.
